channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Hunters carried trout past waterfalls into fishless Norwegian mountain lakes 7,000 years ago
item.description: In a remarkable feat 7,000 years ago, ancient hunter-gatherers transformed Norwegian mountain lakes by introducing brown trout. Archaeologists discovered remnants of intricate fish traps from 5000 BC, highlighting how these early communities intentionally manipulated their ecosystems for sustainable food resources. They even transported live fish through rugged landscapes, paving the way for the fisheries we know today, where anglers still catch their ancient descendants.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Saudi Arabia launches new one-year, multiple-entry Umrah visa with 90-day stay
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian techie in Ohio dies from 'rare, life-threatening condition'; felt severe pain in left leg after dinner
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Florida turns restaurant waste into reefs; fish and dolphins rebound
item.description: Florida restored parts of the Gulf of Mexico by returning more than 500,000 tonnes of recycled oyster and clam shells to the sea between 2007 and 2024. The shells helped rebuild oyster reefs, improve water quality and support the return of fish, turtles and dolphins to levels not seen in over 25 years.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Weaponisation of water': Pakistan seeks Canada's help to revive Indus Waters Treaty with India
item.description: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar requested Canadian support for the Indus Waters Treaty. He stated India suspended the agreement, adding a new dynamic to security. Dar urged India to end water weaponization and uphold international law. India maintains its position remains in abeyance due to terrorism. Pakistan views the Indus as a lifeline and will defend its sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Waiting for their arrival': Iran dares US ground invasion on Kharg Island
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Nasa explores human hibernation for Mars, but major challenges remain
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Frankford Avenue Bridge: The oldest bridge in the US still carrying traffic was built in 1697 and has survived more than 320 years of history
item.description: Philadelphia's Frankford Avenue Bridge, built in 1697, remains in continuous use. This stone arch structure is the oldest surviving roadway bridge still carrying vehicles. It served as a vital link for colonial travel and witnessed revolutionary events. The bridge has been widened and modified over centuries to accommodate modern transport. Designated a National Historic Civil Engineering Landmark, it preserves a connection to America's colonial past.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump says Qatari-gifted Boeing 747-8 will be taken out of service to be 'maxed out'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why did Clavicular break up with Lily Snyder? Streamer's shocking split leaves viewers stunned
item.description: Streamer Clavicular confirmed his breakup with Lily Snyder during a live broadcast. He cited manipulative and volatile behavior as reasons for the split. An incident involving his ear surgery recovery also contributed to the decision. The streamer mentioned ongoing conflicts with his content creation style. Their relationship had previously faced numerous difficulties and near-breakups.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 'A new path for Britain': Andy Burnham becomes 7th UK PM in 10 years, promises stability after Starmer exit
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Brunello Cucinelli: The billionaire building a Universal Library in Italy for the next 1,000 years with free access to humanity's greatest books, inspired by the ancient Library of Alexandria
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'My work is done': Keir Starmer bows out as PM, says UK 'stronger & fairer'
item.description: Keir Starmer has resigned as the United Kingdom's prime minister after two years in office. Andy Burnham will now formally become Britain's prime minister following Starmer's departure. Burnham takes office after replacing Starmer as Labour leader on Friday. The parliamentary system allows for such leadership changes without a general election. Burnham pledged to restore political stability and make politics less toxic.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: PayBae and Lacy controversy explained after questionable Streamer University 2026 interaction
item.description: Streamer University 2026 saw controversy involving Twitch streamer PayBae and Nick Lacy. A video showed PayBae pulling Lacy's injured leg, causing him pain. Lacy questioned why his injury was not respected during the incident. PayBae apologized but felt the situation was being amplified by Lacy's audience. She also claimed a prior "fake beef" influenced her reaction.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Biohacker Bryan Johnson claims ice packs boost male fertility: Does science agree?
item.description: Bryan Johnson, a longevity influencer, claims that icing his testicles greatly increases his sperm count. His unique approach has inspired several Redditors to experiment with this method for improving fertility. Although some research hints at the benefits of mild scrotal cooling for male fertility, experts caution that there is insufficient evidence to suggest significant improvements in testosterone or overall sperm production.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Scientists find toxins from Florida's toxic algae blooms travel through air, raise risk of ALS
item.description: Recent research reveals the presence of cyanobacterial toxins in the air of Southwest Florida, matching those found in dolphins displaying Alzheimer's characteristics. This airborne contamination indicates that inhabitants may be exposed to lower concentrations of these toxins, which are believed to heighten the chances of ALS and various ailments. Unlike food-related toxins, mitigating exposure through breath poses a significant and complex challenge.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: HasanAbi says he may file a restraining order against Destiny after latest livestream comments
item.description: In a troubling turn of events, HasanAbi is weighing the option of filing a restraining order against fellow creator Destiny due to what he describes as continuous harassment stemming from Destiny's supporters. He has accused this community of orchestrated harassment and doxxing attempts. HasanAbi insists that this issue is not just about him but is essential for the protection of others.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian-origin scientist Satchin Panda resigns from California's Salk institute amid sexual misconduct probe
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
